summary: The session focused on the historical figures Eliza Gardner and her family, including Joseph Miller and Lavalette Miller. The user sought clarification on familial relationships and provided corrections regarding the identities and professions of these individuals.
facts: Eliza Gardner was born on June 1, 1831, in Barboursville, Cabell County, Virginia (now West Virginia). Eliza Gardner was the daughter of Marie Therese Raison de La Geneste Gardner and Joseph Gardner. Eliza Gardner attended Miss Sarah P. Vose's Young Ladies' School in Boston, Massachusetts, in 1850. Eliza Gardner contributed articles and poetry to various newspapers and magazines, including the Anglo-African Magazine and the New York Tribune. Eliza Gardner was involved in social and political causes, including abolitionism and women's suffrage. Eliza Gardner passed away on January 15, 1890, in Washington, D.C., and was buried in Spring Hill Cemetery in Huntington, West Virginia. Joseph Miller, the son of Eliza Gardner, was born on August 17, 1848, in Barboursville, Cabell County, Virginia (now West Virginia). Joseph Miller was a lawyer who served as the Prosecuting Attorney for Cabell County from 1877 to 1880. Joseph Miller was appointed United States Attorney for the Southern District of West Virginia by President Grover Cleveland in 1893. Lavalette Miller was born on July 3, 1878, in Hagerstown, Washington County, Maryland. Joseph Miller and Florence Tice were the parents of Lavalette Miller.
